raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assemblywoman Schiavo's Community Water Protection Bill (AB 1631) aims to reshape California's approach to managing water resources.",
    "The Community Water Protection Bill provides the public with the opportunity to address concerns related to water projects' impact on their community.",
    "The CEMEX project near Santa Clarita has been pending with the State Water Resources Control Board for over three decades.",
    "CEMEX would be the largest aggregate mine in North America.",
    "Concerns about water resources have arisen during the three decades since the CEMEX mine's application was initially submitted.",
    "The CEMEX mine would be located along the Santa Clara River, which is one of the last natural rivers in Southern California.",
    "AB 1631 enables community members to provide up-to-date concerns to the State Water Resources Control Board.",
    "Shifting dynamics in water availability due to climate change are relevant to water management decisions.",
    "The legislation emphasizes transparency and public engagement in California's water management."
  ]
}
```
